data.title: Bartels sees S&P 500 ending next year at 7,500 with tech leading
data.description:
Key points:
  • Main US indexes advance; Dow up ~1.5%
  • All S&P 500 sectors green; Healthcare out front
  • Dollar, gold flat; crude, down ~2%, bitcoin down ~3%
  • US 10-Year Treasury yield down to ~4.07%

BARTELS SEES S&P 500 ENDING NEXT YEAR AT 7,500 WITH TECH LEADING

Mary Ann Bartels, chief investment strategist at Sanctuary Wealth, expects the S&P 500 to reach 7,500 by the end of 2026 and that technology stocks will maintain their leadership.

The S&P 500 was last at about 6,625.

"The equity market remains in a global secular bull market as we enter 2026," she writes in a 2026 outlook note Friday.

While she expects volatility in 2026, she sees sector rotation "to and around" growth and value, but sees growth staying in the lead. In addition, she expects tech and tech-related stocks to maintain their leadership.

"Semiconductors, we believe, remain the key leadership industry with insatiable demand driven by data centers – the new main infrastructure being built in the United States," she writes, adding: "the Value areas of Healthcare and Energy, which have significantly lagged in performance, have improved – but we don’t expect a change to leadership by these sectors."

An index of semiconductors is up about 29% for the year to date.

Stocks are not in a bubble, she says, but the market may be moving into "an early bubble period."

"When we overlay the S&P 500 with the equity market from the 1920s, the current market is tracking the pattern from 100 years ago. So, we believe we are moving into the early phase of what will eventually become a bubble. Within a secular bull market, some of the greatest returns come in the last years of that trend – up 166% on average over the last five years."

Worries about exuberance in the artificial intelligence trade and high valuations have pressured stocks lately.

data.title: Peru orders arrest of ex-prime minister under Mexico asylum in Lima
data.description:
A Peruvian Supreme Court judge on Friday ordered the arrest and five-month preventive detention of former Peruvian Prime Minister Betssy Chavez, who is currently under asylum at the Mexican embassy in Peru's capital Lima.

Chavez briefly served as prime minister during the administration of ousted and jailed former President Pedro Castillo. She is being prosecuted for the alleged crime of conspiracy against the state and participation in Castillo's attempt to shut down Congress at the end of 2022.

Prosecutors are seeking a 25-year prison sentence for Chavez, who denies having known of Castillo's plan.

The arrest order, issued on November 18 by Supreme Court judge Juan Carlos Checkley and published on Friday, was sent to the national police and Interpol, according to a court resolution seen by Reuters.

Mexico granted Chavez asylum in early November, prompting Peru to sever diplomatic relations with Mexico. Peru's Foreign Ministry has said it will consult with the Organization of American States before deciding whether to grant safe conduct to the former prime minister.

Castillo, who is in preventive detention and on trial for rebellion, defended Chavez during a court hearing on Friday and rejected the accusations against him.

“I am not here to ask for acquittal. I am not here to ask for clemency or sympathy. I am here to demand justice,” he said during the trial, which began in March and is now in its final stages.

Chavez, also a former legislator, had been in prison since June 2023 and was released in September to stand trial while free.

data.title: Palo Alto-Chronosphere Synergies Questioned Despite M&A Record — Market Talk
data.description: The compatibility between Palo Alto Networks and Chronosphere, which it's buying for $3.35 billion, might be less than its other major acquisition of CyberArk, BMO analyst Keith Bachman writes. The tech synergies are unclear, especially if, as management has suggested, Chronosphere continues to operate independently, and BMO sees most observability buyers in IT operations, not the cybersecurity market. But Palo Alto might be able to avoid the fate of other companies who've unsuccessfully tried to move into the observability market, he said. Splunk, ServiceNow, and Cisco all tried and failed to run an observability play, either by buying underperforming companies or failing on execution. Chronosphere's core business is very solid, Bachman said, and Palo Alto has a strong M&A reputation. (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

data.title: Citadel crypto deal lucratively tames the frontier
data.description:
By Pranav Kiran

The market-maker is investing in coin exchange Kraken at a $20 bln valuation. It gels with boss Ken Griffin’s backing of EDX, which corrals liquidity. Pulling them closer could help smooth out a still-clunky market and possibly open up yet more valuable orders for Citadel.

Full view will be published shortly.

CONTEXT NEWS

Cryptocurrency exchange Kraken said on November 19 that it has confidentially filed for an initial public offering in the United States.

The company said on November 18 that it had raised $800 million at a $20 billion valuation, up 33% in under two months.

Investors including Jane Street, DRW Venture Capital and HSG led the primary tranche. Citadel Securities agreed to invest a subsequent $200 million at a $20 billion valuation.

data.title: U.S. Oil Rig Count Rises By 2 to 419 — Market Talk
data.description: 1337ET - The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S rose by two this week to 419, and was 60 below the year-ago level, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. Despite lower rig counts, U.S. crude production is at record levels with recent EIA weekly estimates showing output above 13.8 million barrels a day. "The traditional link between rig activity and output has weakened recently," the EIA said in a report. "Operators have been focusing on the most productive plays, drilling longer lateral lengths to access more hydrocarbons, and using more efficient completion techniques to ensure economic viability." Rigs directed at natural gas rose by two this week to 127, or 28 more than a year-ago, according to Baker Hughes. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: PLATTS: 100--Beaver Valley-1 nuclear unit in Pennsylvania operating at 30% capacity
data.description: (Platts)--21Nov2025/138 pm EST/1838 GMT **Unit shut in October for refueling, maintenance **Average US nuclear refueling outage lasts 36.7 days Vistra Operations Co.'s 1,011-MW Beaver Valley-1 in Shippingport, Pennsylvania, was operating at 30% capacity early Nov. 21, the US Nuclear Regulatory Commission said. The unit was shut Oct. 12 for refueling and maintenance after being at 87% capacity and in coastdown to refueling Oct. 11, NRC said. Nuclear power units are typically connected and supplying power to the electrical grid after they reach about 15%-20% of capacity. Vistra, however, did not immediately reply to a request for comment sent Nov. 21. The unit was operating at 30% early Nov. 21 and was at zero early Nov. 20, NRC said in daily reactor status reports. According to data from Platts, part of S&P Global Energy, the average length of US nuclear refueling outages in 2024, including two extended outages, was 36.7 days. The 1,008-MW Beaver Valley-2 was operating at 100% capacity early Nov. 21, according to NRC. — Michael McAuliffe, ci.support@spglobal.com-- Edited by Valarie Jackson, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Morris Kandinov LLP Investigating The Wildermuth Fund (WESFX); Investors with Losses Encouraged to Contact Firm
data.description:
San Diego, California--(Newsfile Corp. - November 21, 2025) - National law firm Morris Kandinov has recovered millions of dollars for shareholders of closed-end funds and is recognized as a Top 25 Firm in the 2024 ISS Securities Class Action Services Report. If you purchased shares of The Wildermuth Fund (NASDAQ: WESFX) (NASDAQ: WEFCX) , contact leo@moka.law.

Morris Kandinov informs investors that a class action lawsuit has been filed on behalf of investors of The Wildermuth Fund. According to the filed complaint, The Wildermuth Fund was a mutual fund which purported to provide investors access to a diversified portfolio of asset classes, including private equity. Wildermuth Advisory, LLC served as the Fund's investment adviser. On June 29, 2023, the Fund announced that, based upon the recommendation of the adviser, the Board of Trustees had approved a plan of liquidation for the Fund. On November 1, 2023, as part of liquidation, the Board replaced the advisor with a new investment advisor. Following this replacement, the new investment advisor undertook a review of the Fund's assets. It determined that the fair values of several of the Fund's investments were significantly lower than previously calculated. By 2024, the Fund's net asset value was revised to less than $2.00 per share, an 80% reduction from its prior levels. To learn more about this investigation and your rights, visit: https://moka.law/case-contact-form/. All representation is on a contingency fee basis. Shareholders pay no fees or expenses.

data.title: ESALQ Citrus Analysis
data.description: citrus analysis CITRUS/CEPEA: Exporters get tariff relief; tahiti lime prices move down Cepea, 21 - The export sector of citrus products has gained an important tariff relief over the last days. The government from the United States has exempted the additional tariff of 10% for the orange juice at the end of last week. As for byproducts (essential oils, therapeutic byproducts and orange pulp), the 10%tariff continues, but they were exempted from the 40% tariff. This is good news for the sector, since it faces a slow pace of OJ shipments, especially to Europe. The European Union has been purchasing less than the usual. Therefore, the reduction of costs to send the product to the US market may favor sales of the Brazilian product, compensating the smaller demand from Europe. Partial data about 2025/26 exports indicate that, without the increase of US purchases, the performance would be even worse, since the North-American market has been currently purchasing higher volumes than Europe. TAHITI LIME - Prices are moving down in the domestic market, influenced by the high supply. The quality of fruits available is considered low, which also presses down values. The price average is at BRL 25.19 per 27.2-kg box this week, dropping 18.17% compared to the period before. Quotations of the tahiti lime to export have averaged BRL 30.81 per box, 13.39% down. For the Portuguese version please refer to or click on <ESQ/CIT/ANALYS>

data.title: Intuit Seen Outperforming on Conservative Guidance — Market Talk
data.description: Intuit backed its fiscal-year guidance after posting strong F1Q results and guiding for a healthy 2Q, setting it up to outperform in the back half of the fiscal year, Stifel analysts say in a note. The company is also set up well as its TurboTax Live offering continues to gain share, boosting growth in its tax business, while its global businesses services unit should sustain growth in the mid-to-upper teens due to strong services usage and a transition upmarket, the analysts say. "Conservative 2H set-up makes for an outperformer," the analysts say, noting Intuit remains one of their top large-cap picks. Intuit is up 5.6%. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: PLATTS: 310--PJM Interconnection prices slide on Intercontinental Exchange - 21Nov25
data.description: (Platts)--21Nov2025/134 pm EST/1834 GMT On-peak power prices for Nov. 24 delivery in the PJM Interconnection footprint tumbled on the Intercontinental Exchange Nov. 21, despite an expected rise in demand. Two PJM hubs posted declines of roughly $13. AEP Dayton moved to $48/MWh, and West Hub fell to just below $52/MWh. West Hub's real-time peak number also dipped by $13, shifting its price to just above $51.50/MWh. PJM expected peakload to hit 99.3 MWh on Nov. 24, a 4.5% gain from its Nov. 21 expectation. CustomWeather anticipated average regional temperatures at 46.2 degrees Fahrenheit to open the workweek. Chicago, Philadelphia and Pittsburgh should all peak around 55 F on Nov. 24, according to the National Weather Service. The Midcontinent Independent System Operator's Indiana Hub posted a decline of about $2.50, dropping its price to $52/MWh. MISO forecast a 51% rise in Nov. 24 wind-power generation from its Nov. 21 expectation, but solar-power output was anticipated to drop 21% in that window. Average MISO regional temperatures to open the workweek stood at 45.2 F, according to CustomWeather, up 1.3 degrees in three days. Highs in Cincinnati and Indianapolis should hit the high 50s, the National Weather Service forecast. The Southwest Power Pool's South Hub day-ahead peak number moved to $40/MWh, up $1.50. The increase was fueled by an expected 5.2% rise in demand to 32.4 GWh from its Nov. 23 number. CustomWeather forecast SPP-area temperatures at a 55.7-degree average for Nov. 24, up 4.1 degrees in 72 hours. Wichita, Kansas, and Oklahoma City will open the workweek with highs in the 60s, according to the National Weather Service. — Michael Fox, michael.fox@spglobal.com-- Edited by Karina Roman, newsdesk@spglobal.com--Platts Electricity Alert--

data.title: Donaldson Company Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend

MINNEAPOLIS--(BUSINESS WIRE)--November 21, 2025--

Donaldson Company, Inc. today announced its Board of Directors declared a regular cash dividend of 30.0 cents per share. The dividend is payable December 22, 2025, to shareholders of record on December 5, 2025. Donaldson is a member of the S&P High-Yield Dividend Aristocrats Index and calendar year 2025 will mark the 30(th) consecutive year of annual dividend increases. The Company has paid a cash dividend every quarter for 70 years.

About Donaldson Company, Inc.

Founded in 1915, Donaldson is a global leader in technology-led filtration products and solutions, serving a broad range of industries and advanced markets. Diverse, skilled employees at over 150 locations on six continents partner with customers — from small business owners to R&D organizations and the world's biggest OEM brands. Donaldson solves complex filtration challenges through three primary segments: Mobile Solutions, Industrial Solutions and Life Sciences. Additional information is available at www.Donaldson.com.

data.title: Cisco Co-Founder Sandy Lerner Lists Virginia Farm for $19.8 Million — WSJ
data.description:
By Katherine Clarke

When technology entrepreneur Sandy Lerner bought Virginia's Ayrshire Farm in 1996, the early-20th century manor house had been uninhabited for roughly a decade. The pipes had burst, the basement was flooded with water, and the ceiling's crown moldings were cracked and crumbling.

"The joke was that if you stood in one place long enough, something would hit you on the head," said Lerner, co-founder of the networking-equipment company Cisco Systems.

She paid $7 million for the roughly 800-acre farm outside Washington, D.C., and spent three years and tens of millions of dollars fixing it up. At the same time, she left Silicon Valley to become a full-time organic farmer. Ayrshire Farm, where she raises cattle, chickens and other livestock, was among the first in Virginia to achieve both Certified Humane and USDA Certified Organic status.

"Everybody says to me, 'Why did you move to Virginia?'" Lerner said. "My answer is, 'Because I could.'"

Now that she is slowing down at age 70, Lerner said, she is putting 571 acres of the farm on the market for $19.8 million.

Located in Upperville, about 60 miles outside of Washington, D.C., Ayrshire's stately manor house was built for Brigadier General James A. Buchanan, Lerner said. Fieldstone quarried on the estate was used to build the roughly 17,000-square-foot residence. When completed around 1911, she said, it had luxuries such as electricity, indoor plumbing and central heating. However, the house was left largely unused after 1987, when Buchanan's granddaughter died, according to the family.

For Lerner, purchasing Ayrshire marked a dramatic turning point in her life. After co-founding Cisco in 1984 with her then-husband, Leonard Bosack, she had made a name for herself in Silicon Valley as a powerful female tech entrepreneur. But she was fired from the company after it went public in 1990. For a while she stuck around the Valley, founding the cosmetics brand Urban Decay, which has since been acquired by L'Oréal.

But Lerner, who divorced Bosack in the early 1990s, felt unsettled despite her success. "I was sort of restless," she said.

Her solution was unorthodox: She left behind her high profile in business to focus on organic farming.

The decision wasn't as arbitrary it might initially seem. For one, Lerner had grown up in a ranching family in Northern California. "I've always been an animal nut," she said, recalling how she used to lie on the family's steers while doing her homework. She was active on the farm from age 6, she said, mucking out barns while her friends were "fixing their hair."

While at Cisco, she traveled frequently to trade shows on the East Coast and fell in love with the mid-Atlantic region.

She looked at close to 50 properties in the region before settling on Ayrshire, which she was advised to avoid because it was in such bad shape. She didn't listen. The naysayers "had the wrong vampire," she said.

Lerner started the Ayrshire renovation by restoring the stables on the property. Once an avid jouster at Renaissance fairs, she had come from California with four shire horses, and she wanted to make sure they were situated first. Meanwhile, she lived in a mouse-infested bedroom in the main house.

At the time, the 14-bedroom house was configured with a "strange warren of rooms," she said. She replaced a section of the house with a glass-walled conservatory shipped from England.

She also restored moldings and other original details; the library and butler's pantry retain their original working safes, for example.

While Lerner poured herself into restoring the manor house, her acquisition of Ayrshire was always more about farming. At the farm's peak, Lerner had about 2,000 head of cattle and was raising about 20,000 chickens each month. The animals aren't included in the sale price, but a buyer could potentially purchase them separately.

Lerner said she is selling Ayrshire because she is looking to simplify her life. While she used to live in the manor, in recent years she has spent most of her time in a smaller cabin on an adjacent 222-acre parcel of land, which she plans to keep.

"It's an easy house to live in, but it does get a little bit weird with just one person," she said of the larger manor.

Ayrshire is one of the most significant properties to come on the market in Virginia horse country in recent years, said listing agent Daniel Heider of TTR Sotheby's International Realty, who is co-listing the property with colleague Laura Farrell.

Lerner said she is hopeful that a prospective buyer might also take over the farming operations, though she admits it isn't lucrative.

"I had to go make money to support my farming habit," she said.

Write to Katherine Clarke at Katherine.Clarke@wsj.com

data.title: Ford Motor Co and Novelis Provide Update on Oswego Plant Incident
data.description:
On November 21, 2025, Ford Motor Co and Novelis announced the resumption of operations at the Oswego plant after a fire incident.

Key Highlights:

  • Fire at Novelis Oswego plant was contained with no injuries reported.
  • Cold mill and heat treatment operations have resumed at the plant.
  • Novelis to build a new plant in Alabama, operational by late 2026.
  • Ford maintains 2025 EBIT guidance of $6-6.5 billion.
  • Ford projects 2025 free cash flow of $2-3 billion.

Original SEC Filing: FORD MOTOR CO [ F ] - 8-K - Nov. 21, 2025

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: REG - Power Metal Res. - Directions Hearing for Share Capital Reduction
data.description:
RNS Number : 6214I Power Metal Resources PLC 21 November 2025  

21 November 2025

Power Metal Resources PLC

("Power Metal" or the "Company")

Directions Hearing for Share Capital Reduction

Power Metal Resources Plc (AIM: POW, OTCQB: POWMF), the London listed natural resources exploration company and project incubator with a global project portfolio, provides an update on the hearing for directions ("Directions Hearing") in the High Court of Justice, Business and Property Courts of England and Wales, Companies Court (the "Court") which was attended by representatives of the Company today in connection with the Company's application to approve the reduction of its share capital ("Share Capital Reduction").

The Company notes that that the claim was adjourned for hearing on 9 December 2025 (the "Confirmation Hearing") when any creditor or shareholder of the Company who desires to object may attend and be heard.

The Company will make further announcements in due course as appropriate.

Background

As announced on 19 September 2025, the Company is proposing a Share Capital Reduction in accordance with the Companies Act 2006. The Share Capital Reduction, once confirmed by the Court, will create distributable reserves to provide the Company with flexibility for future distributions and corporate purposes.

Expected Timetable

The timetable for the remaining principal events in connection with the Share Capital Reduction are as follows:

Confirmation Hearing

9 December 2025

Expected date for the Share Capital Reduction to be effective ("Effective Date")

On or around 24 December 2025

*Each of the times and dates set out in the above timetable are subject to change by the Company, in which event details of new times and dates will be notified by a further announcement by the Company. In particular, the expected date for the Confirmation Hearing to confirm the Share Capital Reduction, and the expected Effective Date of the Share Capital Reduction are based on provisional dates. The dates are also subject to any directions of the Court and the date for the registration of the court order is dependent upon, amongst other things, the date on which the Court confirms the Share Capital Reduction and the date that all filings have been completed by the Registrar of Companies at Companies House.

The Company will make further announcements in due course as appropriate.

For further information please visit https://www.powermetalresources.com/ or contact:

Power Metal Resources PLC

Sean Wade (Chief Executive Officer)

+44 (0) 20 3778 1396

SP Angel Corporate Finance LLP (Nomad and Joint Broker)

Ewan Leggat/Jen Clarke

+44 (0) 20 3470 0470

Tamesis Partners LLP (Joint Broker)

Richard Greenfield/Charlie Bendon  

+44 (0) 20 3882 2868

BlytheRay (PR Advisors)

Tim Blythe/Alastair Roberts

+44 (0) 20 7138 3204

powermetalresources@blytheray.com

NOTES TO EDITORS

Power Metal Resources plc (AIM: POW, OTCQB: POWMF) is a London-listed natural resources exploration company and project incubator which finances and manages global resource projects and is seeking large scale metal discoveries.

The Company has a principal focus on opportunities offering district scale potential across a global portfolio including precious, base and strategic metal exploration in North America, Africa, Saudi Arabia, Oman and Australia.

Project interests range from early-stage greenfield exploration to later-stage prospects currently subject to drill programmes.

Power Metal will develop projects internally or through strategic joint ventures until a project becomes ready for disposal through outright sale or separate listing on a recognised stock exchange thereby crystallising the value generated from our internal exploration and development work.

Value generated through disposals will be deployed internally to drive the Company's growth or may be returned to shareholders through share buy backs, dividends or in-specie distributions of assets.

This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. Terms and conditions relating to the use and distribution of this information may apply. For further information, please contact rns@lseg.com or visit www.rns.com.

RNS may use your IP address to confirm compliance with the terms and conditions, to analyse how you engage with the information contained in this communication, and to share such analysis on an anonymised basis with others as part of our commercial services. For further information about how RNS and the London Stock Exchange use the personal data you provide us, please see our Privacy Policy.  END  MSCFELFAFEISEEF

data.title: Third Regional Treasure in The Great Canadian Treasure Hunt is Released in the Golden Triangle of Northwestern British Columbia
data.description:
The third regional prize, over $34,000 in gold coins, are hidden in the heart of British Columbia's legendary gold mining region

Toronto, Ontario--(Newsfile Corp. - November 21, 2025) - EarthLabs Inc. (OTCQX: SPOFF) (FSE: 8EK0) The Great Canadian Treasure Hunt continues to mesmerize Canadians as thousands come together to search for more than $1.3 million in gold treasure. The organizers, The Northern Miner, are excited to announce the third regional prize: six one-ounce gold coins, valued at over $34,000, are located in one of Canada's most prolific gold regions - the mineral-rich belt of northwestern B.C.'s Golden Triangle.

This third regional prize in the Great Canadian Treasure Hunt follows the discovery of the second regional prize in Cobalt, Ontario, by a father-son duo. These regional prizes continue to drive excitement and momentum among treasure hunters and observers across Canada, not to mention the desire to track down the grand prize.

"The Golden Triangle is more than a patch of mountains in northwestern B.C., it's a living testament to Canada's rich mining legacy," said Anthony Vaccaro, President of The Northern Miner Group. "For over a century, miners have pursued its vast mineral wealth, uncovering some of the richest gold, silver and copper deposits in the world. Today, the Golden Triangle continues to bridge Canada's historic mining achievements with future opportunities, exemplifying the nation's resource potential across the country."

The newly released B.C. treasure invites followers of the hunt to learn more about the Golden Triangle's significant role in Canada's mining history, explore its scenic wilderness and join the search for the treasure. Every regional clue ties back to the grand treasure, valued at more than $1 million, hidden somewhere in Canada.

Watch the Golden Triangle reveal video here: English | French

Participants can join the hunt and view the new Golden Triangle clue at https://www.northernminer.com/news/the-northern-miner-treasure-hunt-golden-triangle-dreams-from-buck-to-billions/1003884857/ or treasure.northernminer.com.

Monthly Prizes Connect to the $1M+ Gold Hunt

Nine more bonus prizes (each representing six one-ounce gold coins) will roll out over the next year, revealed through new monthly stories and videos. Every release carries two layers of mystery: clues pointing to the regional bonus prize and the $1+ million grand treasure. Together, these releases will ignite fresh debates and theories while inspiring people to reconnect with the country's history of adventure and discovery.

This campaign is proudly presented with the support of industry sponsors including Agnico Eagle Mines Limited, Sprott Money, EarthLabs Inc., IAMGOLD Corporation, Kinross Gold Corporation, The World Gold Council, McEwen Inc., Alamos Gold Inc., Ernst & Young LLP, Mining Matters, MINING.COM,

CEO.CA and The Canadian Mining Journal.

For more information, including full contest rules, FAQs and updates, visit treasure.northernminer.com.

Follow @northernminer (X/FB/YouTube) | @thenorthernminer (IG) | @mining (X) | @miningdotcom (IG/FB/YouTube); @ceodotca (X/IG/FB/TikTok) | @ceocafilm (YouTube) for ongoing clues and community updates.

-30-

About The Northern Miner

The Northern Miner is a one-of-a-kind information resource. With over 110 years of experience serving the mining and exploration industry, crucial reports by The Northern Miner writing staff inform the decision-making process of thousands of high-performing mining professionals.

Founded in 1915, The Northern Miner remains the industry's most respected mining news authority, known for its on-the-ground journalism, editorial independence, and deep sector expertise. Now owned by EarthLabs Inc., it operates alongside platforms like MINING.COM, CEO.CA, and Canadian Mining Journal, delivering critical insight and trusted intelligence to the global mining community.

About EarthLabs Inc.

EarthLabs Inc. (OTCQX: SPOFF) (FSE: 8EK0) is a mining investment, technology, and media company that aims to provide strategic leverage to the metals and mining sector through investments, royalties and a full suite of data-driven media SaaS tools and services including CEO.CA, The Northern Miner, MINING.COM, Canadian Mining Journal and DigiGeoData.

For media inquiries, please contact:

Amanda Upshaw

Brookline Public Relations

aupshaw@brooklinepr.com

403-389-2291

Disclaimer

18+. No purchase necessary. Open to residents of Canada only. All prize valuations are in Canadian dollars (CAD) and based on the spot gold prices as of November 20, 2025, and may fluctuate with market prices. Full contest rules, eligibility criteria, and redemption process available at treasure.northernminer.com.

Neither the TSX Venture Exchange ("TSXV"), OTC Best Market ("OTCQX") nor its Regulation Services Provider (as that term is defined in the policies of the TSXV) accepts responsibility for the adequacy or accuracy of this release.

Cautionary Statement on Forward-Looking Information

Certain statements contained in this news release constitute forward-looking statements within the meaning of Canadian securities legislation. All statements included herein, other than statements of historical fact, are forward-looking statements. Often, but not always, these forward-looking statements can be identified by the use of words such as "estimate", "potential", "projected", "assumed", "planned", "to be", "may", "could", "should", or similar expressions.

Forward-looking statements involve known and unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors that may cause actual results, performance, or achievements to differ materially from those expressed or implied. While the Company has attempted to identify key risks and assumptions, actual outcomes may vary.

Forward-looking statements reflect the beliefs, expectations, and opinions of management as of the date of this release. Undue reliance should not be placed on forward-looking statements.

data.title: Google Just Dodged Its Biggest Threat Yet -- And AI May Have Handed It the Lifeline
data.description:
Google (GOOGL) just walked out of one of the biggest antitrust battles in years with surprisingly little damage, and the irony is striking: the same disastrous Bard rollout that once wiped 8% off Alphabet's value may have helped the company avoid deeper regulatory cuts. In 2024, Judge Amit Mehta ruled that Google illegally monopolized search, in part by paying billions of dollars to Apple and Samsung to secure its default status. But a year later, in his 230-page remedies decision, the judge concluded Google would be required to change almost nothing and he tied that outcome directly to the rise of generative AI. He argued that AI could be the new threat that introduces competition, turning what looked like a regulatory trapdoor into a possible long-term advantage for the company.

Inside the courtroom, big-tech leaders painted very different visions of the future. Microsoft's Satya Nadella testified about his fear that Google could extend its search dominance into AI, while Google's Prabhakar Raghavan described losing sleep over young users drifting away from what he called Grandpa Google. Yet scholars like Tim Wu and Cory Doctorow argue that this narrative of AI automatically toppling entrenched giants may be far too optimistic. Their upcoming books describe how monopolies tend to shift from innovation to extraction when competition fades, with Doctorow arguing that Google's search quality has declined for economic reasons an assertion Google firmly disputes. Both authors point to examples across Amazon, Apple and Unity as cases where platforms moved to increase fees and tighten control once market power was secured.

For investors, the broader tension sits inside the AI race now dominated by the Magnificent 7 Alphabet, Amazon , Apple , Meta , Microsoft, Nvidia and Tesla which represent roughly one-third of the S&P 500's value. Judge Mehta described the astonishing amount of capital pouring into AI and suggested limiting Google's distribution of Gemini could disadvantage it. But the supposed wave of new challengers isn't as independent as it appears: OpenAI relies on Microsoft, Anthropic is backed by Amazon and Google, Perplexity counts Jeff Bezos and Nvidia among its funders, and xAI is financed by Elon Musk. Even Apple is opting to integrate Google's AI into Siri rather than build its own. On Acquired, hosts Ben Gilbert and David Rosenthal argued that Google may ultimately outlast its AI rivals because it has fresher data, enormous compute, deep personalization and profits while many AI startups are burning cash with no net income. Whether AI becomes a tool that expands productivity or another force concentrating power may depend on how much genuine competition actually emerges, not how much disruption regulators hope will appear on its own.
